# qa-cli

Local runner for `pagerguild/qa-team`'s persona-driven QA agents.
Invokes the same `act`-driven matrix workflow that CI uses against
any URL, streaming each agent's progress (stdout, REPORT.md,
screenshots) into the team's Supabase reader at
https://qa.guys.dev.guilde.ai.

The script is hosted at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pagerguild/qa/main/qa.py`
and self-bootstraps via `uv run`. No clone needed.

## How it splits up

- **`pagerguild/qa-team`** â€” owns the orchestrator (`scripts/run_qa.py`),
  the reusable workflow (`.github/workflows/qa-matrix.yml`), and any
  cross-cutting personas (e.g. `dashboard-snapshot` exercising the
  reader app itself).
- **Consumer repos** â€” own their own `.qa/<persona>/` definitions
  (one folder per persona: `persona.toml` + `identity.md` +
  `prompt.md` + any fixtures). `qa-cli` reads `.qa/` from the
  **target repo**.
- **`qa-cli`** (this tool) â€” pulls qa-team's workflow + scripts from
  `~/.cache/pagerguild-qa/qa-team` (refreshed from `main` each run),
  copies the target repo's `.qa/` next to them in a temp workspace,
  and invokes `act` on the qa-matrix workflow.

`--target <URL>` is what the agents navigate against in the browser;
`--here` / `--path DIR` / `--repo OWNER/NAME [--branch B]` is just
where the personas + repo metadata come from. They're orthogonal.

## Prereqs

- `uv`, `gh` (authed: `gh auth login`), `doppler` (logged in:
  `doppler login`), `act`, and Docker Desktop running.
- The shell where you run `qa-cli` must be scoped to the **qa-team
  Doppler config**. `qa.py` mints a 1h service token from whatever
  Doppler scope it sees in the current directory:

      cd ~/some-dir
      doppler setup     # interactive: project=qa-team, config=prd
      # or:
      doppler configure set project=qa-team config=prd --scope $PWD

  This is independent of the consumer repo's own Doppler scope.

## Run plainterms's `insurance-agent` against production

The persona lives in `pagerguild/plainterms-dev` on branch
`fix/issue-1562` (PR #1563) at `.qa/insurance-agent/`. Until that
PR merges, point `qa-cli` at the branch directly â€” it will clone
fresh each run:

    uv run https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pagerguild/qa/main/qa.py \
      --target https://app.plainterms.io \
      --repo pagerguild/plainterms-dev \
      --branch fix/issue-1562

Pick a different target as needed:

- Production: `https://app.plainterms.io`
- PR preview: `https://pt-preview-<N>.fly.dev`
- Local dev: `http://localhost:5173` (auto-rewritten to
  `host.docker.internal` so the in-container agent can reach it)

After PR #1563 merges, drop `--branch`:

    uv run https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pagerguild/qa/main/qa.py \
      --target https://app.plainterms.io \
      --repo pagerguild/plainterms-dev

If you have a local checkout of `plainterms-dev` already on the right
branch, use it instead of re-cloning:

    cd ~/src/plainterms-dev   # checked out on fix/issue-1562
    uv run https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pagerguild/qa/main/qa.py \
      --target https://app.plainterms.io \
      --here

## Output

`qa-cli` prints a `run_id=<uuid>` line near the top. Open
`https://qa.guys.dev.guilde.ai/runs/<run_id>` to watch the run live â€”
each persona streams stdout, an evolving `REPORT.md`, and screenshots
into Supabase as it runs, and the page reflects them in near-real-time.
